item.title: remove_items_by_status takes full path while other CRUD helpers take relative filename
item.intent: Backlog CRUD helpers must use a consistent path convention — the current asymmetry is a latent footgun that will cause silent wrong-file operations when new callers are added.
item.description: update_item() and remove_item() accept a relative filename (e.g. 'backlog.json') and prepend BACKLOG_DIR internally. remove_items_by_status() takes a full path directly. Callers today are consistent, but the asymmetry is not documented and will cause bugs when someone adds a new call site with the wrong convention. Fix: change remove_items_by_status to accept a relative filename and prepend BACKLOG_DIR internally, matching the established pattern.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find remove_items_by_status() around line 703 and update it to accept a relative filename like update_item() and remove_item()
Change the jq command argument from '$file' to '$BACKLOG_DIR/$file'
Update cmd_clean() callers (line ~4025-4026) to pass just the filename ('backlog.json', 'bugs.json') instead of the full path variables
Run .aishore/aishore clean --dry-run and then clean on a test setup to confirm no regression
acceptanceCriteria: remove_items_by_status accepts a relative filename consistent with update_item and remove_item
cmd_clean still correctly removes done items after the refactor
grep confirms no other callers of remove_items_by_status exist with full paths
